edges.description: Vessel-wall inflammation can compromise CNS perfusion and cause infarction, focal deficits, and cognitive dysfunction.
Local immune activation is interpreted as an upstream driver or correlate of inflammatory CNS vessel-wall injury.
Large-vessel involvement increases risk of infarction, focal deficits, poor response, disability, and mortality.

nodes.description: Large- and medium-vessel PCNSV is often recognized by angiography and is associated with stroke, persistent focal deficits, infarcts on MRI, and increased mortality.

evidence.reference_title: An update of the Mayo Clinic cohort of patients with adult primary central nervous system vasculitis: description of 163 patients.
evidence.snippet: The patients diagnosed by angiograms more frequently had at presentation hemiparesis or a persistent neurologic deficit or stroke, more frequent infarcts on MRI and an increased mortality.

evidence.reference_title: Adult primary central nervous system vasculitis treatment and course: analysis of one hundred sixty-three patients.
evidence.snippet: large vessel involvement (OR 6.14) and cerebral infarcts at the time of diagnosis (OR 3.32) were associated with a poor response to treatment.

nodes.description: Azathioprine is used as a steroid-sparing maintenance immunosuppressant in CNS vasculitis cohorts after induction therapy, though drug-specific comparative efficacy remains limited.

evidence.reference_title: Vasculitis in the Central Nervous System: Etiology, Characteristics, and Outcomes in a Large Single-Center Cohort
evidence.snippet: Cyclophosphamide, rituximab, azathioprine, and mycophenolate mofetil were utilized as maintenance therapy, often with concurrent prednisone.

nodes.description: Primary CNS vasculitis is limited to blood vessels of the brain and spinal cord. Vessel-wall inflammation can narrow or occlude affected vessels and produce focal or diffuse neurologic deficits.

evidence.reference_title: An update of the Mayo Clinic cohort of patients with adult primary central nervous system vasculitis: description of 163 patients.
evidence.snippet: Primary central nervous system vasculitis (PCNSV) is an uncommon condition in which lesions are limited to vessels of the brain and spinal cord.

evidence.reference_title: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system
evidence.snippet: A rare, medium or small vessel vasculitis characterized by focal and/or diffuse neurologic symptoms due to a documented arteritic process in the central nervous system, in the absence of other identified underlying cause (infectious, systemic, other neurologic diseases, etc.).

nodes.description: Combined glucocorticoid and cyclophosphamide induction is used for more severe PCNSV and is associated with fewer relapses than prednisone alone in the Mayo cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system. Report of 8 new cases, review of the literature, and proposal for diagnostic criteria.
evidence.snippet: Therapy of PACNS with a combination of cytotoxic drugs and high-dose corticosteroids has greatly improved the prognosis for this condition.

evidence.reference_title: An update of the Mayo Clinic cohort of patients with adult primary central nervous system vasculitis: description of 163 patients.
evidence.snippet: Relapses occurred in one-quarter of the patients and were less frequent in patients treated with prednisone and cyclophosphamide compared with those treated with prednisone alone.

evidence.reference_title: Vasculitis in the Central Nervous System: Etiology, Characteristics, and Outcomes in a Large Single-Center Cohort
evidence.snippet: Intravenous methylprednisolone was the predominant induction therapy (63.6%), and cyclophosphamide was the most used adjunctive therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Disease Characteristics and Treatments Associated with Outcome in Primary Angiitis of the Central Nervous System-A Multicenter Cohort Study in 163 Patients.
evidence.snippet: Patients treated with cyclophosphamide alone or in combination with steroids had a lower incidence of relapse than untreated patients

nodes.description: High-dose glucocorticoids are central to induction therapy, either alone in selected cases or combined with additional immunosuppression for higher-risk disease.

evidence.reference_title: Adult primary central nervous system vasculitis treatment and course: analysis of one hundred sixty-three patients.
evidence.snippet: A favorable response was observed in 85% of patients treated with prednisone alone and in 80% of patients treated with prednisone and cyclophosphamide.

evidence.reference_title: European Stroke Organisation (ESO) guidelines on Primary Angiitis of the Central Nervous System (PACNS)
evidence.snippet: The group’s recommendations on induction and maintenance of treatment and for primary or secondary prevention of vascular events also reflect uncertainty due to lack of evidence.

nodes.description: RNA sequencing of PCNSV brain specimens shows molecular heterogeneity with activation of antigen processing, dendritic-cell maturation, neuroinflammation, and immune-cell signatures, supporting local immune activation within CNS vasculitic lesions.

evidence.reference_title: Exploring Gene Expression Profiles in Primary Central Nervous System Vasculitis.
evidence.snippet: Pathway analysis revealed the activation of dendritic cell maturation and antigen processing as well as neuroinflammation in PCNSV versus normal brain, whereas oxidative phosphorylation was inhibited.

evidence.reference_title: Exploring Gene Expression Profiles in Primary Central Nervous System Vasculitis.
evidence.snippet: CIBERSORT estimation of immune cell subsets suggested that activated NK cells, M1 macrophages, memory B cells, and follicular helper T cells were likely to be more prevalent in PCNSV samples.

nodes.description: Downstream CNS injury from inflamed vessels includes ischemic stroke, transient ischemic attacks, hemiparesis, aphasia, cognitive dysfunction, and seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system
evidence.snippet: It presents with non-specific symptoms of headache, stroke or transient ischemic attacks with cognitive impairment, hemiplegia, weakness, and rarely, with cranial nerve involvement, seizures and ataxia.

evidence.reference_title: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system: Clinical profiles and outcomes of 45 patients.
evidence.snippet: The initial presentation was ischemic stroke in 15 (33.3%), hemorrhagic stroke in 4 (8.9%), headache in 11 (24.4%), seizures in 8 (17.8%) and cognitive dysfunction in 5 (11.1%) patients.

nodes.description: Mycophenolate mofetil is reported as a steroid-sparing alternative or maintenance option in adult PCNSV, with favorable disability outcomes in Mayo observational data.

evidence.reference_title: Mycophenolate mofetil in primary central nervous system vasculitis.
evidence.snippet: MMF in combination with GCs achieved a favorable response in most patients.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term remission, relapses and maintenance therapy in adult primary central nervous system vasculitis: A single-center 35-year experience.
evidence.snippet: Mycophenolate mofetil may be an effective alternative to CYC.

nodes.description: Rituximab is supported only by small observational series and literature review data, so it is best represented as a selected refractory or individualized therapy rather than standard first-line treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Rituximab therapy for primary central nervous system vasculitis: A 6 patient experience and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Five of the 6 were refractory to high dose glucocorticoids (GCs) and/or conventional immunosuppressants (IS).

evidence.reference_title: Rituximab therapy for primary central nervous system vasculitis: A 6 patient experience and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Our data support a potential role for RTX treatment in selected patients with PCNSV.
